enrichedRequirements.productRequest: Build a simple field service job tracker where users can create jobs, assign a technician name, update job status, filter scheduled and completed jobs, delete jobs, and see a count of active jobs. It should have a clean browser UI.
enrichedRequirements.enrichedPrompt:
Build a simple field service job tracker where users can create jobs, assign a technician name, update job status, filter scheduled and completed jobs, delete jobs, and see a count of active jobs. It should have a clean browser UI.

--- Proof scenario clarification answers ---
Business: Browser-based field service job tracker for technicians for founders and small teams with clear value proposition and target market.
Users: Dispatchers and field technicians who manage jobs in the browser.
Roles: Single end-user role with staff member access; no separate admin portal for MVP.
Permissions: Users have full CRUD permissions and write access on jobs they create with assign permissions for technicians.
Workflows: Core workflow process covers create job, technician assignment process, job status update stages, scheduled/completed filter, delete job, active job count.
Data: Job entity with id, technician name, status, and schedule fields stored in client state records
Files: No file upload or document attachments required for MVP.
Notifications: No email, SMS, push, alerts, or reminder notifications in MVP.
Integrations: No email integration, calendar sync, or third-party API partner required; product does not integrate with external systems for MVP.
AI: No AI, LLM, chatbot, or recommendation features in MVP.
Monetization: Free productivity tool with no subscription, billing, or payments.
Deployment: Static Vite React SPA for public launch in modern browsers; npm build produces dist/index.html for cloud hosting.
